headline: Week one: vert banked early, calf behaving — mostly
narrative: First week of the twenty-three on the road to Costa Blanca, and the ledger opens well ahead of schedule. The baseline ask was easy vert hikes with downhill reps, and that is exactly what got done — three hike-up, run-down sessions on Alt del Governador, including a double, put the week comfortably past its vert target. The engine side showed up too: a new road bike arrived and immediately earned its keep, capped by a long climb up to Guadalest, with swims filling the gaps.

The calf note from Saturday's run club is the one to watch: some pain on flat running, but by your own read not the grabby kind and in a different spot — encouraging, not clearance. Keep flat running short, and let the hikes carry the vert. Next week asks for longer hike durations and steady engine volume as the target steps up. The overshoot this week is fine as an opener; the habit to build now is consistency, not heroics.
